latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name

Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.

Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

latest.title: Receipt of Funds and Return of Capital
latest.content:
22 Sep 2025 17:20
RNS Number : 3167A
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
22 September 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
22 September 2025
RECEIPT OF FUNDS AND RETURN OF CAPITAL
The Board of Directors of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited is pleased to announce that the Company has received a distribution of US$2.7 million from the V Invest RJ Fund and US$18.59 million from Vision FCVS RJ Fund.
It has been resolved to return an amount of US$13 cents per share ($0.13) to shareholders, being US$18.99 million in total, based on the current number of shares in issue. This return of capital will be effected by way of an issue of redeemable B shares to existing shareholders
pro-rata
to their shareholding on the record date set out below and the subsequent redemption and cancellation of those B Shares.
At the next board meeting (8 October 2025) the Board and the Investment Advisor will discuss the remaining holdings in the portfolio, the potential outcomes and the estimated timing of their monetization. The Investment Advisor will then prepare a detailed shareholder report which will be released via the Exchange, detailing the discussion and the Board's recommendation to shareholders with regards to the Company's future.
It is expected that the timetable for the return of capital will be as follows:
25 September 2025
Ex date
26 September 2025
Record date for shareholders on the register to receive B shares
29 September 2025
B shares allotted and issued to shareholders on the register as at the Record date
30 September 2025
B shares redeemed and cancelled
31 October 2025
Redemption payment made
All redemption payments will be paid gross of tax.
Enquiries
Tim Gardner, Hindsight Solutions Limited
Tel. +44 7917 630 460
Email:
timgardner@hindsightsolutions.co.uk
Website:
​
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/
Apex Fund and Corporate Services (Guernsey) Limited
Telephone: +44 (0) 20 3530 3600

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a specialist investment vehicle that gathers capital from institutional investors and deploys it into a diversified portfolio of assets, primarily focusing on real‑estate and infrastructure projects. It then distributes profits back to shareholders through dividends and capital returns.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The firm taps into a market that still seeks stable, long‑term income from physical assets. By sourcing capital from pension funds and insurance companies, it can lock in large sums that are then used to acquire or refinance high‑quality properties and infrastructure assets, which tend to offer predictable cash flows and low correlation with equities.
company_30s.current_state: The company appears to be in a consolidation phase, having recently returned capital to shareholders and updated its portfolio holdings. It has not yet announced any major new acquisitions, but its latest half‑year report indicates a modest improvement in operating income and a healthy cash balance. The stock is trading well below its historical peak, suggesting a deep discount to its underlying asset value.

rns_narrative: The AGM outcome and the latest net‑asset‑value factsheet show that the firm is returning capital to shareholders and keeping a sizable cash cushion. This signals a focus on preserving capital and rewarding investors, rather than pursuing aggressive growth.

The receipt‑of‑funds announcement confirms that the company has recently raised money, likely to fund a new project or refinance existing debt. Combined with the portfolio update, this points to a shift toward a more balanced allocation between income‑generating assets and growth opportunities.

The half‑year financials reveal that operating margins have stabilized, and the company’s cash‑flow generation is improving. Together, these events suggest the firm is tightening its risk profile and preparing for a more disciplined investment strategy.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter is visible; the few posts that exist are neutral or mildly optimistic, mainly reflecting the fact that the firm is returning capital.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears note that the company’s recent focus on capital return rather than growth could signal a lack of new opportunities, and some analysts worry that the firm may be stuck in a low‑yield environment.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has remained largely silent. A few neutral posts appear when new RNS releases come out, but there is no sustained discussion or trending sentiment.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no recent spikes in search interest.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming quarterly earnings report could reveal a new project or a higher dividend payout, both of which would boost investor confidence.
A significant volume spike at the current support level (around the 200‑day moving average) would indicate that the market is starting to price in a recovery.
If the company announces a new infrastructure deal that adds a high‑yield asset to the portfolio, that could be a trigger for a sharp price move.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 3‑week volume surge above the 20‑day average, a breakout above the 50‑day moving average, and any mention of a new asset acquisition in the next RNS. These would confirm that the company is moving out of capitulation and into a recovery phase.
expect_next.timing_regime: Given the lack of recent trading data, the stock is likely still in the early stages of its crash‑recovery cycle. The APEX compression score suggests a tight range, so a breakout could happen quickly once a catalyst is confirmed.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the firm can pull in a new high‑yield project or lift its dividend, the stock could climb to about 50% of its pre‑crash level. That would double the current price, delivering a 100% upside while the downside risk is capped by the capital cushion the company has built up.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the company may not find enough attractive projects to offset the low‑yield environment, keeping returns stagnant. If the firm continues to return capital without adding new income sources, the price could stay near its current low, giving a modest 10–15% upside at best.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 100% if the price hits half its pre‑crash value; downside is limited to the current level because the firm has a strong cash position and a history of returning capital.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the 50‑day moving average with at least 20% volume above the 20‑day average. Size the position to risk no more than 5% of your portfolio on this trade, and set a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear announcement of a new project or a dividend increase in the next RNS. If the company issues a press release that mentions a new infrastructure deal, consider entering immediately as that is the most direct catalyst for a price jump.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. The firm is not facing delisting, bankruptcy, or fraud allegations.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield Group sits at a deep discount after a sharp decline, but with a solid cash base and upcoming earnings that could reveal new assets; the risk/reward is asymmetric – a modest loss is limited, while a bounce to half its pre‑crash level would double the investment.
